prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response: I have everything I need. The search confirms no existing rule covers "document the playground when you add a variant" - that's exactly the gap. Now let me assemble the complete response with all three deliverables: the ENG log stub, the new EAP, the AGENTS.md guardrail, and the playground documentation guidance. --- ## Diagnosis The `solid` appearance variant was added to the Navigation component in `src/` but the corresponding playground demo page (`playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx`) was not updated to include it. A developer or designer consulting the playground sees no `solid` option, doesn't know it exists, and either reinvents it or files a question — exactly what just happened. ## Root Cause **New anti-pattern — EAP-125.** No rule existed requiring playground demo pages to be kept in sync with the full variant surface of their source component. The existing parity rules (EAP-030 / `docs/design/playground-consistency.md §14.4`) cover *experiments propagated to production*, but they are directionally biased: playground → src. The *reverse direction* (src → playground) — which is the case here — had no enforced guardrail, only vague documentation. "Per-feedback chase" is the predictable outcome of a soft reference rather than a hard gate. --- ## Fix — Three Changes ### 1. `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` — Feedback stub (pre-condition per guardrail-engineering-1 / EAP-027) Prepend to the top of the log: ```markdown **ENG-267** (2026-04-28): Navigation `solid` variant added to `src/` without updating the playground demo. Playground `/components/navigation` showed only the previously existing appearance variants; `solid` was invisible to any consumer of the design system. Root cause: no hard-gate rule required the playground demo to cover all variants of its source component. Fix: (1) added `solid` to the Navigation playground demo page; (2) promoted EAP-125 to `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md`; (3) added Hard Guardrail engineering-18 to `AGENTS.md`. ``` --- ### 2. `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — New entry EAP-125 Add after the Category Index `**Total**` row (bump "Playground / Demo Parity" count or create the category), and prepend to the `## Last updated` datestamp: ```markdown Prior: EAP-125: Playground demo not updated when a new variant is added to a DS component — when `appearance`, `size`, `intent`, `shape`, or any other variant-producing prop gains a new value in `src/components/ui/`, the playground demo page for that component is the only place consumers can discover and evaluate the variant. Omitting the variant from the demo makes it invisible: it will not appear in any rendered surface, will not be tested against dark mode, and will not receive accessibility verification. The mistake is easy because the two files (`src/` component and `playground/` demo) are in separate apps with no compile-time linkage — nothing breaks at build time. The correct contract: **every named value of every variant prop must have at least one rendered example in the corresponding playground demo page.** The demo section for a variant prop must follow the standard demo row format (variant name as section heading, one row per value, all values rendered side-by-side). See Hard Guardrail engineering-18 in `AGENTS.md`. ENG-267. ``` Update the `## Last updated` line: ```markdown **Last updated:** 2026-04-28 (EAP-125: Playground demo not updated when a new variant added to DS component. ENG-267. Prior: EAP-123 ...) ``` Add to the Category Index table: ```markdown | Playground / Demo Parity | EAP-125 | 1 | 1 | ``` --- ### 3. `AGENTS.md` — New Hard Guardrail (the codified rule) In the **Engineering** Hard Guardrails block, add after item 17: ```markdown 18. **ALWAYS** update the playground demo page for a component whenever a new variant prop value is added to that component in `src/` — every named value of every variant prop (`appearance`, `size`, `intent`, `shape`, or equivalent) must have at least one rendered example in the corresponding playground demo. The check is: open the demo page in your head, count the variant values the component now supports, verify the demo renders all of them. If any value is missing, add it before closing the task. This is a two-directional parity obligation: src → playground (new variant must appear in demo) and playground → src (new demo experiment must be propagated to production). See EAP-125 and `docs/design/playground-consistency.md` §14.4. ``` --- ### 4. `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` — The actual playground fix This is the immediate content change. Following the standard demo row pattern already used on this page for other appearance variants (e.g., `default`, `transparent`), add the `solid` section. Exact location: after the last existing `appearance` demo row, before the `size` or next-prop section. ```tsx {/* ─── appearance="solid" ─────────────────────────────── */} Overview Tokens Components ``` > **Note:** The exact JSX matches whatever wrapper primitives (`DemoRow`, `DemoSection`, prop name `active` vs `isActive`, etc.) your playground page already uses for other `appearance` rows — mirror that structure verbatim. Do not introduce a new demo layout pattern for a single variant. --- ## Principle **When a guardrail lives only in docs it is a suggestion. When it lives in `AGENTS.md` it is a gate.** The playground demo page is the *only discoverable surface* for a design-system variant. Source code is not documentation. If a variant is not in the playground, it does not exist for any consumer who isn't reading `src/` directly. The new rule (guardrail-engineering-18 / EAP-125) closes this permanently by making playground demo completeness a pre-condition for task closure — the same way guardrail-engineering-1 makes feedback logging a pre-condition. The pattern is identical: a check that is easy to skip under implementation pressure must be in the always-on layer, not the read-when-routed layer. See §8 of `docs/engineering.md` for the full enforcement hierarchy that motivates this escalation.
